channel.title: Reverb — New Bass Guitars
channel.description: Recently listed bass guitars on Reverb

item.title: Epiphone Thunderbird '64 Bass Guitar 2024 - Ember Red — $600
item.description:
$600 · Excellent · Mike's Music Gear

2024 Epiphone Thunderbird '64 Bass - Ember Red (Excellent)

Up for sale is a beautiful 2024 Epiphone Thunderbird '64 Bass in Ember Red. It looks and plays like new, but I am selling to downsize my instrument collection. Custom Epiphone factory soft case included.

Specifications & Features

Right-handed

34" Scale

Body Material: Mahogany

Neck Profile: 1960's rounded bass

Pickups: Gibson ProBucker Humbuckers

Condition Notes

Functional: 100% operational

Cosmetic: Excellent condition

Shipping

This guitar will be professionally packed and fully insured via Reverb Safe Shipping. A signature will be required upon delivery. Please message me with any questions!

item.title: Warwick Corvette standard 2004 - Bubinga — $1,910.31
item.description:
$1,910.31 · Very Good · Panagiotis Stef

Warwick standard corvette in Bubinga made in Germany in 2004.

Upgraded electronics from passive to active with MEC active preamp and a pair of MEC j set in black chrome.Also upgraded the hardware from silver to black.


All the original electronics and hardware will be included.


Comes with rockbass flightcase.

item.title: Epiphone Jack Casady Signature Bass with Laurel Fretboard 2024 - Metallic Gold — $650
item.description:
$650 · Excellent · Mike's Music Gear

2024 Epiphone Jack Casady Signature Bass - Metallic Gold (Excellent)

Up for sale is a beautiful 2024 Epiphone Jack Casady Signature Bass in metallic gold. It looks and plays like new, but I am selling to downsize my instrument collection. Custom Epiphone guitar case included.

Specifications & Features

Right-handed

34" Scale

Body Material: Maple

Neck Profile: Rounded "C"

Pickups: Humbucker

Condition Notes

Functional: 100% operational

Cosmetic: Excellent condition

Shipping

This guitar will be professionally packed and fully insured via Reverb Safe Shipping. A signature will be required upon delivery. Please message me with any questions!

item.title: Baby Bass Corvann 5 strings 2015 — $2,800
item.description:
$2,800 · Excellent · Wilber Leonardo's Gear Locker

Baby bass Corvann 5 strings

Modelo 2015

item.title: Fender Custom Shop 64 Jazz Bass, Relic, Sunburst with Hard Case — $4,203.23
item.description:
$4,203.23 · Excellent · Conor's Gear Emporium

A stunning Fender Custom Shop 64 Jazz in a custom sunburst relic finish. One owner from new and in amazing condition. Comes with a case candy, certificates and original Fender Custom Shop hardcase. Stacked pots as per '62 Jazz, its an amazing bass. Purchased less than a year ago in mint condition, fully setup with fresh strings. Happy to discuss lower offers. thanks

item.title: Schecter Custom - Natural — $599.99
item.description:
$599.99 · Excellent · Supernova Music Memphis

Great bass! Sounds and plays great! No case.

item.title: Warwick 6 String Thumb Bass BO Bolt On 1997 Walnut Body Red Stain — $1,500
item.description:
$1,500 · Fair · Dean's Gear Garage

1997 Warwick BO-6 with walnut body and wenge neck with a red stain. With the best version of brass nut, in my opinion, and all original electronics. This bass has been played and modified over the years to account for neck dive (very common on these small bodied instruments. It has a metal tube screwed into the top horn and the amazing thing is..it works!) I believe the bass was struck at some point on the body by the bridge (see photo) because the original bridge was bent. This has been replaced but I will include all original hardware anyway. The bass was drilled before me for another kind of neck dive adjustment but I have filled in these holes with the belief that some day I would have the body re-done. Some scuffing on the body's stain and a few screw holes for straps on teh rear of the body. The neck, however, is perfect and the bass sounds great as Warwicks should. She is rather "an angel with a dirty face" at this point, but 100% playable and useable and sounds incredible. Please note that the strap will be included (Levy's 4 inch) because the way the horn adjustment works at present the strap cannot be removed unless the horn extender is un-screwed. One tab on the rear compartment case lid is broken off but the lid stays on just fine. Own a classic and rare Warwick for a fraction of an unblemished specimen...one that can either be fixed up cosmetically or played without worrying about dents and dings. By the way, the original hardshell case will also be included. I am always available to answer any questions!

item.title: Egmond Bass or Tenor Guitar hot single coil P90 style Pickup 11 ohm — $95
item.description:
$95 · Very Good · Robert's House of Psychedelic Nonsense

Vintage 1960s Egmond bass guitar pickup. 11 ohm. Nice hot, classic P90 single style. Tested and ready to install on your vintage piece. You could possibly use this on a tenor guitar.

item.title: Fender Jazz bass 2023 - Miami blue — $999.99
item.description:
$999.99 · Excellent · Supernova Music Memphis

Beautiful bass! Looks new. Comes with gig bag.

item.title: 2013 Fender American Deluxe Jazz Bass — $1,250
item.description:
$1,250 · Very Good · Cosmogear

Awesome Jazz light and lively. The lightest Fender I've had at 7.5 lbs. Really cool white blonde finish where you can still see the ash woodgrain. Its been played a bit but still very clean shape with original case and paperwork ,truss rod tool, case key etc.

item.title: Warwick Streamer stage 1 - Dark red — $1,999.99
item.description:
$1,999.99 · Good · Supernova Music Memphis

Plays great, sounds amazing! See photos for condition. Lots of small dings and scratches. This is a player!

item.title: Hondo Professional II 1980’s - Black — $650
item.description:
$650 · Good · TechEx

Solid Hondo II Professional bass. In good shape, very functional. Stock DiMarzio pickups. Floating hi mass bridge (added two bits of copper foil for spacing). Some oxidation on chrome and light scratches on the glossy black body.
Some dings shown in photos. The neck dings are fairly smooth and don’t hinder playability. Frets are excellent, plenty of life left.
Straight solid neck, excellent late 70’s/early 80’s player.
Packed solid and shipped quickly using Reverb’s Safe Ship protection.

item.title: Fender Standard Precision Bass with Rosewood Fretboard 1991 - 1997 - Crimson Red Metallic — $575
item.description:
$575 · Very Good · Liam's

Comes with hard shell case as well as locking tuners and original bridge, with bridge replacement mounted to bass, the only notable damage is the dent shown in the back of the bass

item.title: Fender Dimension 5 String Bass USA 2013 - Black — $1,095
item.description:
$1,095 · Very Good · Don’s Gear Locker

Probably my last Fender Bass up for awhile, at least. This is a 2013 Fender Dimension 5 String Bass Made in USA I’ve had this one a couple years and have played it quite a bit. Weights about 9.6 pounds. Active Electronics, High Mass Bridge, a really great player. The Online shop that I bought it from said it included a hard case. I guess everyone has their own idea of what that means. The case was so bad I would have not even put Roadkill in it. Tossed it. Got this Brand New Fender Hardcase for it. This is the one for the P/J thing, but it fits very securely and there is very little if any fore and aft, or side to side movement. Very nice fit.Some of the pictures were purposely taken with LED overhead lighting to show up any marks and scratches. We should all look this good. Headstock front has some finish checking. Back of Neck and Headstock are satin, this is the way I got it. Included, but not pictured, in the case pouch are an extra set of Ernie’s .130’s , the top half for the Dunlop Straplocks, and some extra Batteries. It’s a package deal, you can’t cheap out here and not want something. Packed and ready to go, so no other pictures are available. Sorry.

item.title: Ibanez MODE MDM1300 4-String 2026 - Amber Burst - Mint — $997.60
item.description:
$997.60 · Excellent · James's Gear Locker

Mint condition.

Weighs approximately 8.5 pounds.

Balances and plays beautifully.

Only used in my smoke-free home studio.

Ibanez padded gig bag included.

item.title: Ibanez Black/White Eagle Bass 2015 - White — $1,100
item.description:
$1,100 · Very Good · TechEx

2015 Ibanez Black Eagle reissue professionally refinished in Olympic white. Looks killer, plays great. Upgraded Leo Kwan Badass II bridge. Upgraded Seymour Duncan Quarter Pound Jazz pickups. Action and intonation are excellent, frets are perfect. Save for a few barely noticeable dings this bass is in overall great cosmetic and functional condition. Comes with a 70’s Ibanez “chainsaw” hard case that’s in good shape as well.

I pack using plenty of bubble wrap and heavy duty boxes. Items are sent via Reverb’s “safe ship” service which protects both buyer and seller. Someone must be available to sign for the item on delivery.

item.title: Baby Bass Corvann 5 string 2015 — $2,800
item.description:
$2,800 · Excellent · Wilber Leonardo's Gear Locker

baby bass 5 strings Corvann bass modelo 2015 en perfectas condiciones..

item.title: Ernie Ball Music Man Stingray 5 HH 5 String Bass — $2,585
item.description:
$2,585 · Excellent · Mass Musik

Excellent condition, no scratches or blemishes, original Ernie hardshell .

item.title: Mayones Jabba Custom 5: Eye Poplar Top, Swamp Ash Body, Velvetrone Pickups & Musashi Preamp — Handcrafted in Poland — $3,900
item.description:
$3,900 · Excellent · FretSpot

Mayones Jabba Custom 5: Eye Poplar Top, Swamp Ash Body, Velvetrone Pickups & Musashi Preamp — Handcrafted in Poland

Mayones has been building world-class instruments in Gdansk, Poland since 1982, and the Jabba Custom is their flagship Jazz-style bass design executed at the highest level of their custom shop. This particular example is a stunning build with a spec sheet that rewards careful reading: every detail was chosen intentionally and the result is a bass that is as visually distinctive as it is tonally versatile.

The Eye Poplar 3A top in Antique Red Raw is the first thing that stops you. The raw finish lets the wood breathe and develop over time, and the Eye Poplar figure has a three-dimensional depth that photographs cannot fully capture. The headstock matches in Antique Red Raw while the back of the body and neck are finished in Trans Natural Matt, giving the whole instrument a cohesive two-tone aesthetic that is elegant without being over the top. Swamp Ash body delivers that characteristic snap, tight low end, and lively midrange that makes a Jazz-style bass cut through any mix.

The maple fingerboard with acrylic red and black swirl block inlays and Super Blue Luminlay side dots is genuinely one of the most visually striking fingerboard combinations I have seen. Ferd Wagner nickel silver frets, dual carbon rod reinforcement in the one-piece maple neck, 34.25" scale, and a 20" radius make this a fast and stable playing instrument. Bolt-on construction, Mayones Bigfoot bass bridge with 19mm string spacing, and Hipshot Ultralite Clover tuners with 4+1 configuration round out the hardware.

The electronics package is exceptional. Velvetrone Muscle 5 bridge pickup and Velvetrone Gallant 5 neck pickup running through the Musashi Nitōryū 3 preamp: volume with push/pull active/passive switching, balance, middle, treble/bass controls, and a mini-switch for serial/single coil/parallel voicing. Switchcraft output jack, AUX input, headphone jack, and Schaller security locks. This is a genuinely comprehensive and professional electronics package.

At 8 lbs 13 oz this is a comfortable and lightweight instrument for a 5-string. Mint condition, ships with the Mayones gig bag and original certificate of authenticity.

Specs:

  • Model: Mayones Jabba Custom 5
  • Serial Number: JAB2501324
  • Production Number: 0349/2025/10
  • Origin: Handcrafted by Mayones Luthiers, Gdansk, Poland
  • Construction: Bolt-On
  • Body: Swamp Ash
  • Top: Eye Poplar 3A (Antique Red Raw)
  • Body Back Finish: Trans Natural Matt
  • Neck: 1-Piece Maple, Two Carbon Rods
  • Neck Finish: Trans Natural Matt
  • Headstock: Eye Poplar 3A (Antique Red Raw)
  • Fingerboard: Maple
  • Fingerboard Radius: 20"
  • Frets: Ferd Wagner Nickel Silver (2.64x1.20)
  • Scale Length: 34.25"
  • Markers (Front): Acrylic Red & Black Swirl Blocks
  • Markers (Side): SB-20 Super Blue Luminlay Dots
  • Binding: None
  • Pickup Configuration: J-MM
  • Pickups: Velvetrone Muscle 5 (Bridge), Velvetrone Gallant 5 (Neck)
  • Preamp: Musashi Nitōryū 3
  • Controls: Volume (Push/Pull Active/Passive), Balance, Middle, Treble/Bass
  • Switching: Mini-Switch (Serial/Single Coil/Parallel)
  • Jacks: Switchcraft 1/4", AUX 1/8", Headphone 1/8"
  • Bridge: Mayones Bigfoot Bass Bridge 5-19 (Black)
  • String Spacing: 19mm
  • Tuners: Hipshot Ultralite Clover Black 4+1
  • Nut: Plastic
  • Strap Locks: Schaller Security Lock (Black)
  • Hardware: Black
  • Weight: 8 lbs 13 oz
  • Condition: Mint
  • Includes: Mayones Gig Bag, Certificate of Authenticity

item.title: Fender Vintera II Mustang with two Fender Humbuckers — $1,150
item.description:
$1,150 · Very Good · Ian's Gear Emporium

I purchased two Fender Humbucker Pickups and had a local Luthier put them in a Vintera II Mustang. The pickups are a little different looking (one more dinged than the other) and pretty different sounding. There is a two way switch that was also added so you can hear either pickup or put them together. Feel free to reach out with questions.

item.title: Kiesel B25 Brian Bromberg Signature 5-String in Vintage Sunburst: Neck-Through, Ebony Fingerboard & 18V Active Electronics — $1,650
item.description:
$1,650 · Excellent · FretSpot

Kiesel B25 Brian Bromberg Signature 5-String in Vintage Sunburst: Neck-Through, Ebony Fingerboard & 18V Active Electronics

Brian Bromberg is one of the most celebrated bassists in the world, equally revered in jazz, funk, and rock circles, and the Kiesel B25 was designed in direct collaboration with him to meet his exacting standards. The result is an instrument that blends boutique ergonomics, USA craftsmanship, and serious tonal firepower at a price point well below what you would expect for this level of build quality.

The neck-through construction runs a tung-oiled maple neck with dual carbon fiber reinforcing rods and a fully adjustable dual-action truss rod through the center of the alder body wings, delivering exceptional sustain, stability, and resonance. The ebony fingerboard is fast, smooth, and features Brian's signature 12th fret abalone inlay. 24 medium jumbo stainless steel frets, 34" scale, and 14" radius. The Hipshot A-style bridge with string-through body construction maximizes transfer and punch. Kiesel Radium radiused alnico single-coil pickups deliver deep, smooth bass response with excellent midrange punch and clear, articulate top end.

The 18V active/passive electronics package is one of the most comprehensive onboard preamp systems available on a production instrument: master volume with push/pull active/passive switch, pickup blend, stacked concentric bass and high frequency, stacked concentric mid frequency with sweep control. You can dial in virtually any tone from vintage warmth to modern aggression.

This example is in vintage sunburst finish with black hardware, a visually stunning combination that gives a classic aesthetic to a thoroughly modern instrument. Mint condition at 9 lbs 8 oz.

Specs:

  • Model: Kiesel B25 Brian Bromberg Signature (5-String)
  • Construction: Neck-Through Body
  • Body Wings: Alder
  • Tone Block: Maple
  • Neck: Tung-Oiled Maple, Dual Carbon Fiber Reinforcing Rods, Dual-Action Truss Rod
  • Fingerboard: Ebony, 14" Radius
  • Frets: 24, Medium Jumbo Stainless Steel
  • Scale Length: 34"
  • Nut Width: 1.75"
  • Inlay: Brian Bromberg Signature Abalone at 12th Fret
  • Pickups: Kiesel Radium KRJ Radiused Alnico Single-Coils
  • Electronics: 18V Active/Passive, Master Volume (Push/Pull), Pickup Blend, Stacked Bass/High Frequency, Stacked Mid Frequency/Sweep
  • Bridge: Hipshot A-Style, String-Through Body
  • Hardware: Black
  • Finish: Vintage Sunburst
  • Weight: 9 lbs 8 oz
  • Condition: Mint
  • Gig Bag Included

item.title: Warwick Rockbass Star Bass Flamed Maple 4 2010s - Vintage Sunburst Transparent — $927.70
item.description:
$927.70 · Very Good · The Reverend's sacristy

Hello,


I’m parting with this bass, it’s not originally a fretless, it’s left handed but setup for a right handed player, I’ve added some glow in the dark stickers as side markers, they are lined with the frets.


As you can see it’s a player’s instrument, it play perfectly though 😊.


It comes with a battered Epiphone hardcase.


Feel free to ask your questions.

item.title: Steinberger Spirit XT-2 in Hot Rod Yellow: Headless 4-String, Mint Condition, Hardshell Case Included — $549
item.description:
$549 · Excellent · FretSpot

There has never been anything quite like a Steinberger. When Ned Steinberger introduced his headless bass design in 1979 it was genuinely revolutionary, and decades later the XT-2 remains one of the most distinctive and practical instruments ever built. At just 38.5" long and 6 lbs 5 oz this particular example is featherlight and compact enough to fit in an overhead airline compartment. The headless construction and 3-piece hard maple neck make it virtually impervious to temperature and humidity changes: the kind of stability that touring players dream about.

This one is in Hot Rod Yellow, a finish that is as bold and unapologetic as the instrument itself. Mint condition throughout. The patented DoubleBall bass bridge with 40:1 ratio direct-pull tuning system means you tune it once and it stays there. Full 34" scale with 24 medium jumbo frets, so there is nothing compromised about the playing experience despite the compact format. EMG Select humbucker pickups at neck and bridge with individual volume controls and master tone. Folding leg rest for seated playing comfort. Ships with the original hardshell case, which is a meaningful upgrade over the standard gig bag.

If you have been curious about headless bass design or simply want the most travel-friendly serious instrument available, this is the one.

Specs:

  • Model: Steinberger Spirit XT-2
  • Strings: 4
  • Finish: Hot Rod Yellow (Gloss)
  • Body: Basswood
  • Neck: 3-Piece Hard Maple, Thru-Neck Construction
  • Neck Shape: 1960s SlimTaper D Profile
  • Fingerboard: Engineered Hardwood
  • Fingerboard Radius: 14"
  • Frets: 24, Medium Jumbo
  • Scale Length: 34"
  • Zero Nut Width: 1.50"
  • String Spacing: 0.70" at Bridge
  • Pickups: EMG Select Humbucker (Neck & Bridge)
  • Controls: Neck Volume, Bridge Volume, Master Tone
  • Bridge: Patented Steinberger DoubleBall Bass Bridge
  • Tuning: Patented 40:1 Ratio Direct-Pull System
  • Hardware: Black
  • Other: Folding Leg Rest
  • Weight: 6 lbs 5 oz
  • Condition: Mint
  • Case: Original Hardshell Case Included

item.title: Fender American Standard Jazz 2013 - 3-Color Sunburst — $1,050
item.description:
$1,050 · Excellent · Tim's Gear Bazaar

Look at this Beauty! 2013 Fender Jazz bass in excellent condition! The bass plays and sounds fantastic! It was recently setup by a professional tech and it’s spot on! It weighs a little over 9 pounds but it’s comfortable on the back during gigs! The frets have a bundle of life left on them and the neck is really smooth with a nice gloss finish on it with some beautiful wood grain showing! The fender deluxe case comes with it as well and it’s in excellent condition too! I will only ship within the USA. Thanks for dropping in and have a wonderful day!!

item.title: Moon JJ-4 4-String Bass with Rare Figured Maple Top: Handmade in Japan — $2,600
item.description:
$2,600 · Excellent · FretSpot

Moon JJ-4 4-String Bass with Rare Figured Maple Top: Handmade in Japan

Moon PGM has been building some of the finest handcrafted Jazz-style basses in Japan for decades, and outside of serious bass circles they remain one of the best kept secrets in the boutique world. The JJ-4 is their flagship Jazz Bass design: white ash body, maple neck with deep joint construction, 34" scale and active electronics built to deliver crisp highs, tight lows, and explosive slap attack. These are genuinely world class instruments.

This particular example stands out even by Moon standards. The figured maple top is a rare and non-standard appointment on the JJ-4: standard production models come in solid finishes over ash. A figured maple top on a JJ-4 is a custom or special order build, making this one genuinely unique. At 8 lbs 14 oz it is light, comfortable, and well balanced. Mint condition throughout.

Specs:

  • Model: Moon JJ-4
  • Origin: Handmade in Japan by Moon PGM
  • Body: White Ash
  • Top: Figured Maple (Rare/Non-Standard)
  • Neck: Maple on Maple, Deep Joint
  • Fingerboard: Maple
  • Frets: 21
  • Scale Length: 34"
  • String Spacing: 19mm
  • Pickups: EMG
  • Preamp: EMG
  • Controls: 2 Volume, 2-Band Active EQ
  • Weight: 8 lbs 14 oz
  • Condition: Mint
  • Case: Original Moon Gig Bag Included

item.title: 60's-70's German Jazz Bass Guitar Long Scale 4 String Rellog Gitona — $596.97
item.description:
$596.97 · Good · Saiten&Brass

Hello everyone, I'm selling a really cool electric bass. The bass is fully functional and has a fantastic, rich, jazzy sound. Its patina is truly amazing. The instrument was made in Germany in the 1960s or 70s. Feel free to ask any questions or request more pictures. Make me an offer! Best regards

item.title: Strack Woodworking J-Bass: 100-Year-Old Douglas Fir Body, Handcrafted by Former Lakland Master Builder Dan Strack — $2,600
item.description:
$2,600 · Excellent · FretSpot

Dan Strack is not a name that appears on a lot of people's radar yet, but among those who know, he is regarded as one of the most talented and principled instrument builders working today. His credentials are serious: he served as a master builder at Lakland, one of the most respected American bass brands of the modern era, and continued that work building instruments for Dan Lakin's own brand after Lakland. His philosophy centers on simplicity, honest materials, clean lines, and intentional design. Every instrument he builds is handcrafted one at a time in his Chicago shop.

This Strack J is built from 100-year-old reclaimed Douglas Fir. Dan has written about this wood extensively: Douglas Fir that has been air drying for a century is exceptionally well tempered, stable, and resonant in ways that freshly milled wood simply cannot be. The wood has had a century to settle, and the result is an instrument with a warmth, sustain, and acoustic character that most modern builds cannot touch. Paired with Dan's characteristically shallow C neck profile and carefully curated electronics, this is a Jazz bass that rewards players who appreciate craft and provenance over flash.

At 9 lbs 8 oz it is a solid and well balanced instrument. Mint condition. Ships with gig bag.

Specs:

  • Builder: Strack Woodworking (Dan Strack, Chicago)
  • Model: Strack J (Jazz Bass Style, 4-String)
  • Body: 100-Year-Old Reclaimed Douglas Fir
  • Fingerboard: Birdseye Maple with block inlays
  • Weight: 9 lbs 8 oz
  • Condition: Mint
  • Case: Gig Bag Included
  • Builder Background: Former Master Builder at Lakland and D. Lakin Instruments

item.title: Burny WBS-80 Made in Japan Luna Signature Gold 90s — $764.12
item.description:
$764.12 · Excellent · Michał's Gear Outlet

Burny WSB-80EV Gold Sunburst – Late 90s Japanese Minimalist Bass (Made in Japan)


Up for sale is a rare variant of the Japanese Burny WSB-80EV from the late 1990s. This model was the signature instrument for Luna, the bassist of the rock band Eins:vier, who was quite popular in Japan back then.


The band’s music relied on straightforward, raw rock, and that is exactly the philosophy behind this bass. The setup is extremely minimalist: two active FGI single coils and just a single volume knob. If you ever need a more complex EQ, Fernandes left plenty of room in the electronics cavity for additional pots.


While most examples from this series were made of alder, this particular piece was built using heavy ash (or the dense Asian sen wood variety). The instrument weighs exactly 4.5 kg. The finish is a rare Gold Sunburst—a golden center fading into black edges. The transparent layers of the finish allow the deep, wide grain patterns of the ash to beautifully show through under the light. The neck is maple with a rosewood fretboard hosting 21 frets. The nut width is 40mm, providing a sleek, fast profile that offers a great compromise between a traditional Jazz Bass and a Precision.


Tonally, the dense ash body brings a lot to the table. The bass features a very fast attack, a solid, tight low end, and long, natural sustain. Character-wise it hints at a classic Jazz Bass, so you can easily pull out those punchy Fender-style 'snaps'. The output from the pickups is hot; this axe sits perfectly in the mix when played with a pick, but it responds dynamically to slap as well.


The visual and technical condition is very good. The finish is smooth and glossy, free of major blemishes or chips. The black Gotoh hardware operates precisely and holds tuning perfectly. The neck is straight, the truss rod turns smoothly without resistance, and the original frets are high, level, and do not require any crowning. The string action is currently set low and comfortable at exactly 1.8mm over the 12th fret.


A gig bag is included. Testing and playing the bass is more than welcome in Poznań, Poland. Safe shipping in a sturdy box is also no problem. I am not interested in trades, as I am consistently downsizing my collection. This is a private sale, shipping from Poznań, Poland.

item.title: Strack Woodworking Le Studio P: Handcrafted 4-String P-Bass Built from Reclaimed Le Studio Lumber — $3,299
item.description:
$3,299 · Excellent · FretSpot

This is not just a bass. It is a piece of rock history.

Dan Strack is a Chicago-based luthier who specializes in handcrafted instruments built from reclaimed and sustainably sourced wood. The Le Studio P is built from lumber salvaged from the beams of Le Studio, the legendary recording facility in Morin Heights, Quebec that served as home base for some of the most important rock recordings of the late 1970s and 1980s. Rush recorded a string of landmark albums there including Permanent Waves, Moving Pictures, Signals, Grace Under Pressure, Power Windows, and Hold Your Fire. The Talking Heads, David Bowie, April Wine, Queensryche, and countless others also worked within those walls.

Le Studio was destroyed by fire in 2017. When Geddy Lee was asked about its loss he said it was not only a place where Rush did some of their best work, but where profound personal moments were experienced and enduring friendships were forged. He called it the end of an age.

Dan Strack recovered wood from Le Studio's structure before it was gone and built a small number of instruments from it. This Le Studio P is one of them. The body features a modified P-style cutaway and a poured resin pickguard cast from the same Le Studio beam material. It is a genuinely one of a kind instrument with a documented, specific, and meaningful provenance that Rush fans and rock history enthusiasts will immediately understand and appreciate.

Strack's philosophy centers on simplicity, clean lines, honest materials, and intentional design. The result is an instrument that is as compelling to play as it is to own. At 8 lbs 9 oz it is lightweight and comfortable. Mint condition. Ships with gig bag.

Specs:

  • Builder: Strack Woodworking (Dan Strack, Chicago)
  • Model: Le Studio P
  • Configuration: P-Bass Style, 4-String
  • Body: Reclaimed lumber from Le Studio, Morin Heights, Quebec
  • Pickguard: Poured resin cast from Le Studio beam material
  • Body Style: Modified P-Bass cutaway
  • Pickup: believed to be Fralin
  • Weight: 8 lbs 9 oz
  • Condition: Mint
  • Case: Gig Bag Included
  • Provenance: Built from structural lumber salvaged from Le Studio recording facility, where Rush recorded Moving Pictures, Permanent Waves, Signals, and other landmark albums

item.title: Alembic Rogue 1999 — $5,499
item.description:
$5,499 · Excellent · FretSpot

Late 1990s Alembic Rogue 5-String with Europa Electronics: Neck-Through Construction, Ebony Fingerboard & Hardshell Case

Alembic has been building some of the finest and most distinctive basses in the world since 1971, and the Rogue is one of their most visually dramatic and ergonomically refined designs. The sweeping body curves, extended upper horn that reaches to the 12th fret, and tummy and forearm contours make this one of the most comfortable and perfectly balanced instruments you will ever strap on. There is simply no neck dive with a Rogue, even in five string configuration.

This late 1990s example is in excellent condition and features the Europa electronics package, which is one of the most powerful and versatile onboard preamp systems ever put into a production bass. The Europa electronics center around Alembic's proprietary low-pass filter system: rather than a conventional cut and boost EQ, you are shaping tone by sweeping a filter across the frequency spectrum from around 300Hz all the way up to 6kHz, with a Q-switch that tightens the filter response for a more focused, parametric-style boost at your chosen frequency. Bass and treble cut/flat/boost switches give you instant access to useful tonal shifts on the fly without touching a knob. Volume, pan, filter, Q-switch, bass switch, and treble switch. Once you understand the system it is extraordinarily intuitive and delivers a tonal range that conventional active EQ simply cannot match. Deep and warm for blues and R&B, bright and zingy for slap, aggressive and focused for rock: it is all there.

Neck-through construction, ebony fingerboard, 34" scale, gold hardware, and the characteristic Alembic brass nut and bridge hardware for maximum sustain and string isolation. At 10 lbs 4 oz this is a substantial instrument but the balance is exceptional. Excellent condition throughout. Ships with hardshell case.

Specs:

  • Model: Alembic Rogue 5-String (Late 1990s)
  • Construction: Neck-Through Body
  • Fingerboard: Ebony
  • Scale Length: 34"
  • Nut Width: 1.875" (approx)
  • String Spacing: 18mm (approx)
  • Pickups: Alembic
  • Electronics: Europa Package (Volume, Pan, Low-Pass Filter, Q-Switch, Bass Cut/Flat/Boost, Treble Cut/Flat/Boost)
  • Hardware: Gold
  • Tonewoods: See photos (specific combination to be verified by buyer)
  • Weight: 10 lbs 4 oz
  • Condition: Excellent
  • Case: Hardshell Case Included

item.title: Ernie Ball Music Man Sterling 4 H with Rosewood Fretboard 1994 - Sunburst Maple — $1,844.95
item.description:
$1,844.95 · Very Good · iGear Emporium

Mfg. Aug 23, 1994 in California, USA.

Selling my beautiful Sterling 4H:

- Satin Birdseye Maple neck

- Sunburst Maple finish

- Black Tortoise pickguard

Comes with Gig Bag.

Body and Neck are MINT. Sounds, plays and looks great

item.title: 1998 Fender Precision Bass '70 Reissue vintage white — $1,611.83
item.description:
$1,611.83 · Good · Yeahman's Vintage & Used Guitars

Ca. 1998 Fender Precision Bass '70 Reissue, vintage white. Made in Japan, serial number O031930. This bass is mostly original, except for the knobs, thumb rest and Fender Pure Vintage '60 pickups. Freshly setup with 45-105 strings, shows quite a bit of wear and tear, but has perfect frets. Weighs 4.05kg.

For sale this one warwick  thumb neck through  6 strings ,very special and unique  special order for a famous greek bass player , well balanced , thin neck , bartolini pickups , electronics have been updated  by Greek  Luthier odyssey basses   but i can give also the original mec. made for Warwick  Made in Germany / 91330 Eggolsheim / Franconia

Trade is possible with Yamaha BBNE2 Nathan East Black

item.title: 2025 Fender Japan MIJ FSR Traditional Late '60s Jazz Bass Ice Blue Metallic Matching Headstock — $1,649
item.description:
$1,649 · Excellent · Guitar Chimp

For Sale: 2025 Fender Japan MIJ FSR Traditional Late '60s Jazz Bass Ice Blue Metallic Matching Headstock Block Inlays

Up for sale is a 2025 Fender Japan MIJ FSR Traditional Late '60s Jazz Bass in the stunning Ice Blue Metallic finish with a matching headstock, complete with its original Fender gig bag.

This Factory Special Run is one of the most desirable Jazz Bass models Fender Japan has released in recent years. Inspired by the iconic late-1960s Jazz Basses, it combines vintage styling with modern Japanese craftsmanship, featuring a matching painted headstock, bound rosewood fingerboard with pearloid block inlays, vintage-style pickups, and the exceptional fit and finish Fender Japan has become famous for.

The Ice Blue Metallic finish is absolutely gorgeous, changing character depending on the lighting. Under bright light it has a brilliant icy metallic sparkle, while in softer lighting it takes on subtle blue and silver hues. Combined with the matching headstock, white binding, and block inlays, this bass has an unmistakable vintage custom-color look that is guaranteed to stand out.

Condition

Excellent – 9.5/10

  • Light surface scratches
  • A few very small dings from careful handling

Please review all photos carefully for the most accurate representation of condition.

The bass is 100% original with no cracks, breaks, repairs, or structural issues.

The neck is straight, the original frets show virtually no wear, and the bass has been professionally set up by our in-house luthier. It plays effortlessly with low action, excellent intonation, and no buzzing or dead spots.

The original Fender gig bag is included.

Specifications

  • Model: Fender Japan MIJ FSR Traditional Late '60s Jazz Bass
  • Year: 2025
  • Made in Japan (MIJ)
  • Factory Special Run (FSR)
  • Serial Number: JD25029279
  • Finish: Ice Blue Metallic
  • Matching Headstock
  • Body: Basswood
  • Neck: Maple
  • Neck Profile: Late-'60s "U" Shape
  • Fingerboard: Rosewood with White Pearloid Block Inlays and White Binding
  • Radius: 9.5"
  • Scale Length: 34"
  • Frets: 20 Vintage-Style
  • Nut Width: 1.51"
  • Neck Depth: .84" at the 1st Fret
  • Neck Depth: .96" at the 12th Fret
  • Nut: Bone
  • Pickups: Fender Vintage-Style Jazz Bass Single-Coils
  • Controls: Volume 1, Volume 2, Master Tone
  • Bridge: Vintage-Style 4-Saddle
  • Tuners: Vintage-Style
  • Hardware: Nickel/Chrome
  • Weight: 9 lbs 2 oz
  • Case: Original Fender Gig Bag

Electronics & Features

This Factory Special Run captures everything players love about late-'60s Jazz Basses while adding one of Fender's most beautiful custom colors.

The pair of Fender vintage-style Jazz Bass pickups delivers the unmistakable Jazz Bass sound—tight lows, growling mids, and crisp, articulate highs. Whether played clean or pushed through an overdriven amp, the pickups maintain exceptional clarity and dynamic response.

The traditional dual-volume and master tone controls provide the full range of classic Jazz Bass tones, making this instrument equally at home playing jazz, funk, rock, blues, country, or modern music.

Visually, this bass is simply stunning. The Ice Blue Metallic finish, matching painted headstock, white neck binding, and pearloid block inlays create one of the most iconic Fender aesthetics ever produced. It's a look that immediately evokes the golden era of late-'60s Fender instruments while benefiting from the precision and consistency of modern Japanese craftsmanship.

Tone & Feel

This Jazz Bass delivers everything players expect from a premium vintage-style Fender.

The vintage-style pickups provide outstanding articulation, punch, and note definition while retaining the warm, woody character that has made the Jazz Bass one of the most recorded basses in history.

The Late-'60s "U" shaped neck feels substantial yet comfortable, while the modern 9.5" radius makes playing effortless across the entire fingerboard.

At 9 lbs 2 oz, the bass feels perfectly balanced with excellent sustain and resonance.

Transparency & Photos

As with all Guitar Chimp listings, detailed photos are provided of the exact instrument—no stock photos.

At the end of the photo set, you'll find close-up images taken under direct lighting to highlight any cosmetic imperfections.

We don't just describe the condition—we show it in detail so there are no surprises.

Many customers tell us our guitars often arrive in better condition than expected.

Why Buy From Guitar Chimp?

With over 18 years of experience, Guitar Chimp is trusted by players and collectors worldwide for high-quality, player-ready instruments.

Every bass is thoroughly inspected and professionally set up by our in-house luthier.

We are experts at shipping and have safely shipped thousands of guitars and basses all over the world. Every instrument is professionally packed, fully insured, and shipped with signature confirmation, so you can buy with complete confidence.

📞 Call/Text

💬 Message anytime—we're always happy to help!

This 2025 Fender Japan MIJ FSR Traditional Late '60s Jazz Bass in Ice Blue Metallic is a gorgeous, limited-edition example featuring a matching headstock, block inlays, vintage-inspired appointments, and the exceptional craftsmanship that has made Fender Japan FSR models some of the most sought-after instruments on the market.

🎸 Jerald – Guitar Chimp

Providing The Finest Guitars, Basses & Amps Since 2006

item.title: Spector Euro 4LX Doug Wimbish 2025 - Black Stain Gloss — $2,400
item.description:
$2,400 · Excellent · Bryan Stern

Spector Euro Doug Wimbish Black Stain Gloss

Recreates Doug's iconic 1987 NS-2 with refined specs, including a slim neck carve, custom-wound EMG pickups, and Spector’s Legacy Preamp and a limited aged nitrocellulose finish. This bass blends vintage design with modern craftsmanship for an authentic, broken-in feel.

  • Mother Of Pearl Inlays

  • Custom Wound EMG P/J Active Pickups
  • Spector Legacy/Darkglass Preamp
  • 2 Band Eq
  • 1.5 Nut Width
  • Gold Hardware

item.title: LeCompte REV-5 - Matte Olivine Green Pearl — $2,699
item.description:
$2,699 · Brand New · LeCompte Electric Bass

LeCompte REV-5
34” scale
Alder body
Flat black pickguard
Dual action truss rod, graphite reinforced
Maple neck
Patagonian rosewood fingerboard
Medium jumbo frets
Olivine green pearl flake paint - matte
Hipshot hardware
LeCompte Triple Threat Split- Coil pickup
Passive Volume/tone
La Bella IMA strings
Gig bag

Big sound!

There are a couple of minor bark inclusions on the maple neck. One at the 9th fret, treble side and the other in the headstock. They've been stabilized and filled. They can't be felt. Neck is extremely stable and unaffected.

An outright steal at $2699.

Shipping in the US included.

item.title: Overwater by Tanglewood Aspiration Deluxe 5-String — $489.14
item.description:
$489.14 · Very Good · Carlos from Winchester

Excellent beast of a bass. Incredible sound and playability; only selling due to an upgrade.

Has been a work horse for over ten years and never disappoints.

It is in very good condition, with a handful of minor scratches. It will need new strings soon.

The pre-amp failed two years from new, and was replaced with an original one from Overwater, by Overwater themselves.

The black strap is included. Happy to deliver within around the Solent. Will not post overseas due to brexshit, but happy to talk about it.

item.title: Squier 70s Classic Vibe 2010s - Natural — $325
item.description:
$325 · Very Good · Rockman Guitarworks

Classic four-string electric bass guitar has a natural finish maple body. The maple neck features a maple fingerboard with black block inlays and black binding and is Fender branded but is not a Fender neck. It is equipped with two Fender-designed single-coil Jazz Bass pickups and a four-saddle bridge. The instrument has a black pickguard and chrome hardware, including a Squier-engraved neck plate. It plays like a dream and sounds great too.

item.title: Fender precision bass MIM black label — $650
item.description:
$650 · Very Good · David

New upgraded 4 ply pickguard. New knobs speed dials.
Because these instruments feature the full Fender logo with a small "Squier Series" decal on the headstock, they are often confused with standard Squier models
The "Squier Series" with a "black label" refers to an obscure, transitional line of guitars produced in Mexico by Fender between 1992 and 1998. These genuine Fender guitars utilized leftover American and Mexican parts, but were equipped with cheaper, Asian-sourced hardware to create a budget-friendly option. [1, 2, 3]
The Squier series basses were not Squiers. Fender loaded Fender quality bodies and necks with lower grade electronics from Japan and Mexico that they would have also used in squiers, thus the term "Squier series". They also put better electronics in these same "black logo" basses and put them out at a lower price point then the standard J bass. They were basically a step between Standards and Squiers, but both guitars came with Fender hang tags and Fender serial numbers and were considered in the MIM Fender line and not Squiers. [1, 2]

Here is what you need to know about these vintage hybrid guitars:

  • The Construction: Many of these guitars featured high-quality US/Mexican wood (often Tulip Poplar bodies)

item.title: Ibanez Acoustin Electric Bass SGBE110-ATN - Natural — $299.99
item.description:
$299.99 · Good · Victor Litz Music Center

This Ibanez SGBE110 acoustic‑electric bass delivers smooth, comfortable playability with its medium‑low action setup, making it an excellent choice for both stage and studio work. Cosmetically, it’s in very clean condition, with only light pickguard scratches and a small finish ding on the back of the headstock—purely cosmetic and nothing that affects performance.

Equipped with a built‑in preamp and onboard tuner, this bass is ready to plug in and perform. Whether you’re laying down warm acoustic bass lines or looking for a versatile instrument that handles multiple genres, the SGBE110 brings reliability and tone in a sleek, lightweight package.

If you’re interested, feel free to make us an offer.

Specs – Ibanez SGBE110 Acoustic‑Electric Bass

  • Body: Spruce top with mahogany back & sides
  • Neck: Mahogany
  • Fingerboard: Rosewood
  • Scale Length: 32" medium scale
  • Frets: 22
  • Electronics: Ibanez AEQ‑2 preamp with onboard tuner
  • Controls: Volume, EQ
  • Bridge: Rosewood
  • Finish: Natural gloss
  • Pickup: Under‑saddle piezo

ML

item.title: Allparts Fretless Precision Bass Neck - Vintage Tint — $175
item.description:
$175 · Very Good · David's Gear Garage

Allparts Precision Fretless Bass Neck for sale. Ebony lined fretboard. But will be included and mounting screws if needed. The holes in the neck are solid, just had sanded the down for better fit. I can include the mounting screws as well if you’d like. US shipping only. $25 flat USPS Shipping.
Thank you!

item.title: Squier Classic Vibe 60s Precision Bass Left Handed — $300
item.description:
$300 · Very Good · David's Gear Emporium

This Squier Classic Vibe '60s Precision Bass has defiantly stood the test of time and stood out as a priority bass when it comes to live and studio music. Perfect bass for any and all beginner and intermediate players. It comes stock with a Fender-Designed alnico split-coil pickup that sounds awesome. Personally I prefer the sound of the Indonesian Squire models over the Fenders made in Mexico. The neck is very player-friendly with a slim and comfortable C shape and the radius is 9.5". Also stock with a maple neck and fingerboard and narrow-tall frets, as well as a vintage-style bridge with threaded saddles. Local Pickup is preferred but will gladly ship at the buyers expense.

item.title: Project Bass Guitar 4 String - Blue — $100
item.description:
$100 · Good · David's Gear Garage

Project Bass I started not too long ago. Truss Rod works and I have tuners as well if you‘re interested. US Shipping only.
Thank you!!
